front: What is selenocysteine and how does it differ from cysteine?
back: Selenocysteine (Sec, U) contains a selenol group (-SeH) instead of a thiol (-SH). It is incorporated via a UGA stop codon recoding mechanism requiring a SECIS element in mRNA. The lower pKa of selenol (~5.2 vs 8.3) makes it more reactive at physiological pH.
